title: Nikon Automated Microscopy: Import a JOB .bin file into JOBs Explorer
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"664mc","text":"A short guide on how to import a JOB .bin file into JOBs Explorer within NIS-Elements","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Download or transfer the .bin JOB file from link or external hard drive onto the microscope computer running NIS-Elements
Open JOBs Explorer using one of the following options :
From Docked JOBs Explorer Panel Docked JOBs Explorer Panel
From JOBs Explorer Shortcut JOBs Explorer Shortcut
From Search bar "JOBs Explorer" JOBs Explorer Search
From Top Menu > HCA/JOBs > JOBs Explorer JOBs Explorer
Import .bin file into JOBs Explorer using the Import from File option. Please note this option is only available if a database and project have been created.
Open JOBs Explorer and create Database if a Database is not already present

The general advice is not to save the Database on the same drive as the operating system installation, in most cases Windows is installed on the C Drive, and so creating a database on the C Drive should be avoided. The D drive or E drive usually are fast write speed SSDs, which are more appropriate for JOBs database locations.
Create Project if no Project is not already present
The general advice is to name the project after the experiment being conducted.
creator: Mark Rigby
